description_long: This lamppost features a tall, slender black pole with a flared, ornate base for stability. At the top, a horizontal crossbar supports two lantern-style lamp heads, each with a classic, slightly domed cap and a warm yellow glow, suggesting traditional incandescent or LED bulbs. The lamp heads are symmetrically positioned on either side of the pole, giving the structure a balanced and elegant appearance. The design is reminiscent of Victorian or early 20th-century street lighting, often found in urban parks, sidewalks, or historic districts. The surface appears to be made of painted or powder-coated metal, likely cast iron or steel, with glass or acrylic panels enclosing the light sources. There are no visible decorative scrolls or embellishments beyond the flared base and the finials atop each lamp head.
description: A tall, black, double-headed lamppost with classic lantern-style fixtures and a flared base, emitting a warm yellow light. The design is elegant and symmetrical, suitable for urban or park settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing both lantern-style lamp heads and the full crossbar.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the slim profile, with only one lamp head visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying both lamp heads and the crossbar.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile with a single lamp head.
attribute_lamp_head_design: Classic lantern-style with domed caps and finials, featuring warm yellow illumination.
